architecture.summary: Tree of Thoughts (ToT) is a prompting framework that augments language model inference with deliberate tree search over coherent text units ('thoughts'). The LM generates candidate thoughts at each tree node, evaluates them via self-reasoning (value scoring or voting), and a search algorithm (BFS or DFS) explores the tree with lookahead and backtracking. Information flows from problem input → thought generation (prompted sampling or sequential proposal) → state evaluation (LM-based scoring) → search algorithm selection → final output. No model training occurs; all components use the frozen LM via prompting.

biblio.title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models

claims.statement: in Game of 24, while GPT-4 with chain-of-thought prompting only solved 4% of tasks, our method achieved a success rate of 74%
ToT with breadth b=1 achieves a success rate of 45%, while b=5 achieves 74%
ToT generates more coherent passages (7.56) than IO (6.19) and CoT (6.93) on average in Creative Writing, as measured by GPT-4 zero-shot evaluation score

claims.dataset: Creative Writing (100 tasks, blind human study)
claims.statement: in human evaluation of Creative Writing, humans prefer ToT over CoT in 41 out of 100 passage pairs, while preferring CoT over ToT in only 21 pairs
in Mini Crosswords, ToT achieves a word-level success rate of 60% and solves 4 out of 20 games, compared to IO and CoT which achieve less than 16%
around 60% of CoT samples already failed the task after generating the first step in Game of 24, highlighting issues with direct left-to-right decoding
iterative-refine method requires groundtruth feedback signals about equation correctness to work, whereas ToT operates without such external supervision

ToT lacks mechanisms to handle tasks for which GPT-4 already excels, and explores relatively simple tasks that challenge GPT-4; more complex real-world tasks like coding, data analysis, and robotics present opportunities for future work
